goal: Identify 3-5 high-demand, underserved content ideas for History vs Hype

evidence_sources: Web search: 'Plenty of videos repeat it' but 'haven't found debunk videos'
Multiple competing myths documented (Caesar 48 BCE, gradual decline, Caliph Umar fabrication)
Time Magazine, National Geographic, World History Encyclopedia coverage = mainstream interest
hypotheses.underserved_reason: High repetition of myth, very few quality debunking videos with primary sources. Gap between academic consensus (gradual decline, NOT dramatic burning) and popular YouTube content.
hypotheses.channel_fit: Strong - 'both extremes wrong' (NOT Caesar burning, NOT Caliph Umar, NOT single event). Primary sources: Show competing ancient accounts. Modern relevance: 'Religion vs Science' narrative still uses this myth.
hypotheses.modern_hook: Science vs religion debates still cite burning as example of religious anti-intellectualism, but historical reality more complex
hypotheses.script_outline_notes: Act 1: Popular myth (dramatic burning set humanity back 1000 years). Act 2: Show primary sources - multiple accounts, contradictions. Act 3: Academic consensus = gradual decline, NOT single event. Why this myth persists.
hypotheses.next_validation_needed: Check Google Trends, find specific YouTube videos with view counts, verify academic sources available (NotebookLM upload test)

evidence_sources: Web search: Myth widely perpetuated, limited academic debunking on YouTube
Wikipedia: 'Scarcely a Christian scholar of Middle Ages who did not acknowledge sphericity'
Origins documented: Washington Irving 1828 biography of Columbus created dramatized scene
hypotheses.underserved_reason: Widely repeated myth, but quality debunking with primary sources rare. Educational content exists but may not show medieval manuscripts proving sphericity.
hypotheses.channel_fit: Strong - Show medieval manuscripts with Earth sphericity. 'Both extremes wrong': NOT flat Earth believers, NOT Church suppression. Modern relevance: Still cited in 'Dark Ages' narratives about Western civilization.
hypotheses.modern_hook: Still shapes narratives about Western civilization, religious vs scientific thinking, 'Dark Ages' misconceptions
hypotheses.script_outline_notes: Act 1: Columbus myth. Act 2: Show medieval texts (Bede, Aquinas, etc.) knowing Earth spherical. Act 3: How Irving created myth in 1828, why it persists in ideological debates.
hypotheses.next_validation_needed: Google Trends, specific YouTube video analysis, check medieval manuscript availability (academic sources)

evidence_sources: Treaty review possible starting 2048 (22 years away)
Mineral development prohibition up for debate
7 countries with territorial claims (Australia 42% of continent)
Congressional Research Service report, academic papers on governance post-2048
hypotheses.underserved_reason: Limited YouTube content on 2048 deadline, most coverage focuses on current Antarctic science not future territorial disputes. Deadline approaching creates urgency.
hypotheses.channel_fit: Good - 'Deadline approaching' pattern. Show 1959 treaty text, territorial claim maps. Modern relevance: Climate change making Antarctica accessible, mineral wealth, geopolitical competition.
hypotheses.modern_hook: 2048 deadline approaching, climate change making continent accessible, mineral resources, new geopolitical competition
hypotheses.script_outline_notes: Act 1: 2048 deadline, what's at stake. Act 2: History of territorial claims (show maps), 1959 treaty freezing claims. Act 3: What happens after 2048, competing interests.
hypotheses.next_validation_needed: Search for existing YouTube coverage, verify academic sources (treaty texts, CRS reports), Google Trends for 'Antarctica 2048'

hypotheses.topic: Crusades as Defensive War - Historical Debate
evidence_sources: Active scholarly debate documented (4 historians panel, 3-to-1 vote)
Catholic sources claim defensive, academic historians dispute characterization
Multiple articles from Crisis Magazine, Catholic Answers, HuffPost Religion, ISI
hypotheses.underserved_reason: Lots of partisan content (Catholic apologists vs critics), but limited neutral 'both sides wrong' analysis with primary sources from Crusade chronicles.
hypotheses.channel_fit: Excellent - Perfect 'both extremes wrong' topic. Show primary sources (Crusade chronicles, papal bulls). Modern relevance: Still cited in religious conflict debates, civilizational clash narratives.
hypotheses.modern_hook: Still invoked in modern religious conflict debates, 'clash of civilizations' rhetoric, political justifications
hypotheses.script_outline_notes: Act 1: Modern debate (apologists vs critics). Act 2: What Crusaders themselves said (primary chronicles), what Pope Urban II said (Clermont speech). Act 3: Why 'defensive' framing is anachronistic, how both sides misuse history.
hypotheses.next_validation_needed: YouTube content analysis, academic source availability (Crusade chronicles in English translation), Google Trends

evidence_sources: CNN, Euronews, France24 coverage of anniversary
hypotheses.underserved_reason: Recent documentary produced, but may lack forensic primary source analysis. Opportunity for deeper dive into legal history (UN resolutions, treaties).
hypotheses.channel_fit: Moderate - Fits territorial dispute genre. Could show UN resolutions, treaty documents, legal status of Northern Cyprus. Modern relevance: EU membership implications, ongoing negotiations.
hypotheses.modern_hook: 50-year anniversary (2024), ongoing EU membership complications, recent diplomatic efforts
hypotheses.script_outline_notes: Act 1: 1974 division, what happened. Act 2: Legal documents (show UN resolutions, treaties), competing claims. Act 3: Current status, why still unresolved 50 years later.
hypotheses.next_validation_needed: Check if topic too recent (documentary already covered?), academic sources, YouTube video performance data

progress_summary: Phase 1 complete. Phase 2 starting: Narrowing to top 4 ideas based on channel performance data analysis.

channel_performance_insights.belize_breakout: 28,110 views (44x avg), 7.57% CTR, 35.3% retention - territorial disputes with ICJ cases = proven formula
channel_performance_insights.dark_ages_short: 1,383 views - medieval myth topic has audience interest
channel_performance_insights.crusades_covered: 657 views for 'Primary Sources Destroy Crusades' - topic already addressed, moderate performance
channel_performance_insights.genre_validation: Territorial disputes with modern stakes (ICJ, deadlines) = best performers

top_4_final_candidates.rationale: Highest demand + most underserved. NOT covered by channel yet. Perfect 'both extremes wrong' topic. Academic sources confirmed available.
Follow-up to breakout hit (Belize 28K views). Pending ICJ case = modern relevance. Underreported. Same proven formula.
Territorial dispute genre proven. 2048 deadline = urgency. Underserved (limited YouTube coverage). Show treaty maps.
Related to 'Dark Ages' content (1,383 views). Widely perpetuated, limited debunking. Show medieval manuscripts.
